title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: Experts believe that the mountain was formed millions of years ago.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive with perfect infinitive (is thought to have been).

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'm sorry I forgot your birthday,’ said Julia.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with prepositions and gerunds.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't study hard, so I didn't pass the exam.
questions.beforeGap: If I had studied harder, I
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for past hypothetical results.

questions.firstSentence: I am certain that he was not the one who stole the money.
questions.afterGap: the one who stole the money.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction.

questions.firstSentence: The match had just started when the lights went out.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with negative/restrictive adverbs (Hardly had... when).

questions.firstSentence: It is much cheaper to travel by bus than by train.
questions.explanation: Tests comparative structures with 'not as... as'.
